_meta.description: Fallback cache of real NAIC regulatory references for the Regulatory Browser Agent (Agent 4) when Grok 4.3 live-web fetch is unavailable. URLs and titles are real NAIC publications; summaries are paraphrased.

bulletins.title: Model Bulletin on the Use of Artificial Intelligence Systems by Insurers
bulletins.key_requirement: Insurers using AI systems must maintain a written AIS program governing fairness, accountability, compliance, transparency, and safety, and must provide consumers with notice when AI materially affects an adverse decision.
bulletins.summary: Adopted Dec 2023; defines insurer responsibilities for AI governance (FACTS). Requires documented controls, third-party model oversight, and consumer notice on adverse AI-influenced decisions. Adopted or in-progress in 40+ states as of 2026.
bulletins.facts_relevance: Primary authority for the NexusMesh Guard FACTS governance layer.

bulletins.title: Unfair Claims Settlement Practices Act (Model #900)
bulletins.key_requirement: Prohibits 14 unfair claims practices including failure to acknowledge claims promptly, failure to adopt reasonable investigation standards, and failure to provide a reasonable explanation for denial.
bulletins.summary: Model law setting standards for prompt, fair, and equitable claims settlement. The Decision Agent checks buckets 2 and 3 of the 14 prohibited practices. Adopted in some form by all states.
bulletins.facts_relevance: Basis for the Compliance (C) check in FACTS and UCSPA conformance notes.

bulletins.title: Risk-Based Capital (RBC) Requirements
bulletins.key_requirement: Carriers must maintain capital above the Company Action Level (RBC ratio of 200% of Authorized Control Level); ratios below trigger graduated regulatory intervention.
bulletins.summary: Formula-based minimum capital standard scaled to insurer risk profile. The Policy Risk Analyzer Agent checks a carrier's RBC ratio against the 200% Company Action Level.
bulletins.facts_relevance: Supports the financial-soundness check in the Policy Risk report.

state_reporting_requirements.trigger: Knowledge or reasonable belief that a fraudulent insurance act has been or is being committed
state_reporting_requirements.reporting_body: Florida Division of Investigative and Forensic Services (DIFS)

state_reporting_requirements.trigger: Reasonable belief of suspected fraudulent claim
state_reporting_requirements.reporting_body: California Department of Insurance Fraud Division

state_reporting_requirements.trigger: Reasonable belief that an act of insurance fraud is about to be, is being, or has been committed
state_reporting_requirements.reporting_body: Texas Department of Insurance Fraud Unit

state_reporting_requirements.trigger: Reasonable belief of suspected fraudulent claim
